projects.title: AI Phone Assistant
projects.description: An intelligent AI-powered phone assistant that handles calls, takes messages, and more using natural language processing. Includes Twilio voice handling, Google Calendar integration, and appointment management. Built with Gemini and deployed at Vetra.
projects.impact: Live in production at Vetra · Twilio + Gemini

caseStudy.problem: Vetra needed a way to handle inbound calls intelligently — take messages, check availability, and book appointments without human staff always on the line.
caseStudy.techStackReason: Twilio for reliable voice; Gemini for natural language understanding; Node.js for one language across the stack and fast iteration.
caseStudy.challenges: Mapping free-form speech to calendar actions, handling edge cases in conversation flow, and keeping latency low for a good caller experience.
caseStudy.myRole: Built the voice pipeline, Gemini integration, and Google Calendar sync; deployed and maintained in production.
caseStudy.futureImprovements: Multi-language support, richer analytics on call outcomes, and optional human handoff for complex requests.

projects.title: NeighbourNetk
projects.description: A full-stack community support platform that allows users to request help and volunteers to respond. Built with React, Node.js, and SQL.

caseStudy.problem: This started from something I saw at my local club: people were always asking for help (lifts, picking something up, quick favours), but it lived across WhatsApp threads and it was easy for requests to get missed or duplicated.
caseStudy.techStackReason: I went with React because I wanted a fast, clean UI that works on mobile. Node.js kept the backend simple to iterate on, and SQL made the request/response state easy to reason about (who asked, who accepted, what’s still open).
caseStudy.challenges: The tricky part wasn’t the CRUD — it was the flow: making it obvious what a volunteer can do vs a requester, and keeping the status changes sane so you don’t end up with two people “accepting” the same request.
caseStudy.myRole: I built it end-to-end: the role-aware screens, the API, and the database structure for users, requests, and responses. I also tuned the UI so you can scan what’s open and act quickly.
caseStudy.futureImprovements: Add notifications so volunteers don’t have to refresh, basic admin/mod tools for the club, and a lightweight way to tag requests (e.g. ‘lift’, ‘errand’, ‘check-in’) to filter the feed.

projects.title: Internet Guardian AI
projects.description: A web audit tool that scans a URL and returns practical security, performance, and SEO findings using Cloudflare APIs through a wrapper service.
projects.impact: Cloudflare-powered audits · security + performance + SEO in one view

caseStudy.problem: I wanted a quick way to check real website health in one place instead of jumping across separate tools for security, speed, and SEO basics.
caseStudy.techStackReason: React made it easy to build a simple results-first UI, and using Cloudflare through a wrapper gave me cleaner inputs/outputs and faster iteration while wiring scan data to the front end.
caseStudy.challenges: Normalizing mixed scan responses into one readable summary and deciding what to show first so users get signal quickly, not just a wall of metrics.
caseStudy.myRole: Built the front-end flow (URL input -> scan -> results), integrated the API wrapper, and shaped the output into actionable sections for Security, Performance, and SEO.
caseStudy.futureImprovements: Add scan history, trend comparison between scans, richer scoring, and export/share options for reports.

projects.title: Catshop
projects.description: Catshop is an online retail platform that enables customers to check the availability of desired items and purchase any quantity of the available products.

projects.title: Functional Assignment
projects.description: FunctionalAssignment is a university register that can query students using many functions such as making a function that querys a student based of their age.

projects.title: Hotel booking and management system
projects.description: Wanderstays is a hotel booking and management system that allows users to book rooms, manage reservations, and handle customer inquiries. Also allows for hotel management to manage rooms, view bookings, and handle customer requests.

caseStudy.problem: Hotels and guests needed a single place to search availability, book rooms, and manage reservations without back-and-forth emails or phone calls.
caseStudy.techStackReason: React for a fast, responsive UI; Node + Express for a simple, scalable API; MySQL for reliable relational data (rooms, bookings, users).
caseStudy.challenges: Keeping booking state consistent, handling concurrent bookings for the same room, and a clear admin flow for hotel staff.
caseStudy.myRole: Designed and built the full stack: React front end, REST API, database schema, and auth for guests and admins.
caseStudy.futureImprovements: Payment integration (e.g. Stripe), email confirmations, and a dashboard for occupancy and revenue.

projects.title: Market-basket analysis
projects.description: Market Basket Analysis using Python, pandas, and mlxtend. Uncovers frequent itemsets and association rules from grocery transaction data. Includes visualizations.

projects.title: Portfolio
projects.description: This site — React, Vite, responsive layout, accessibility-first. Built with clean structure and performance in mind.
